document.title: Post-Market Surveillance Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council
MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8 Guidelines on a medical devices vigilance system (January 2013)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post-market surveillance plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines PMS information sources, responsibilities, risk reassessment thresholds, competent authority communication, PMCF linkage, and PSUR update expectations for the listed Class IIb product.
keyPoints: The PMS plan is based on Article 83 and Annex III Section 1.1 of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
PMS information sources include sales volume, complaints and feedback, literature review, regulatory databases, similar device information, and product quality information.
The product is stated to be Class IIb, with PSUR updated at least annually and reported when required by the notified body.
Trend reporting uses adverse event occurrences against units sold over a 6 month observation period and may lead to risk management, CAPA, EUDAMED upload, and NB notification.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance plan
concepts.evidence: The post-market surveillance system referred to in Article 83 shall be based on this post-market surveillance plan

concepts.name: Technical documentation
concepts.evidence: For devices other than custom-made devices, the post- market surveillance plan shall be part of the technical documentation specified in Annex II.

concepts.name: Periodic Safety Update Report
concepts.evidence: The below information will be reported in PSUR refer to Section 15 of this document.

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: To verify the clinical performance and safety throughout the expected lifetime of our medical device... we specify the methods and procedures of PMCF activities in A-PMCFP-5-001.

concepts.name: Trend report
concepts.evidence: Trend report focus on non-serious incidents and undesirable side effects.

concepts.name: Field Safety Corrective Action
concepts.evidence: Report by manufacturer on serious accidents and FSCA

concepts.name: Unique Device Identification
concepts.evidence: Later, when the UDI system is started, traceability is guaranteed through UDI system.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: perform benefit-risk analysis and reassessment of risk management when certain indicators reach their respective thresholds.

evidence.claim: The document is Rev.3 dated December 31, 2024.
The document applies to A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The listed detectors are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: PMS data are analyzed for new risks related to performance and safety in the PSUR.
evidence.evidenceText: The data from PMS are analysed whether there are new risks related to the performance and safety of our devices or similar devices in PSUR (A-PSUR-5-001 Periodic Safety Update Report)

evidence.claim: Risk assessment divides severity and probability into five stages.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk assessment divides severity and probability into 5 stages, records them in the risk management report, and manages them according to risk management procedures.

evidence.claim: The user complaint threshold for physical damage is 1 time per year.
evidence.evidenceText: User Complaint Threshold Values: 1 time / 1 year

evidence.claim: Trend reporting focuses on non-serious incidents and undesirable side effects.
evidence.evidenceText: Trend report focus on non-serious incidents and undesirable side effects.

evidence.claim: If likelihood and severity exceed previously estimated values, risk management and CAPA are implemented and the trend report is uploaded to EUDAMED with NB notification.
evidence.evidenceText: If the analysed likelihood and severity are higher than the previously estimated values, risk management and CAPA are implemented and trend report is uploaded to EUDAMED and inform NB.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer reports or manages serious accident and FSCA reports, periodic summary reports, trend reports, PSUR, and FSN through EUDAMED.
evidence.evidenceText: The following information is reported/managed through the electronic system (EUDAMED). Report by manufacturer on serious accidents and FSCA Periodic summary reports by the manufacturer Manufacturer’s summary report on trend (Trend Report) PSUR (Class III and Class IIb implantable products) FSN by the manufacturer

evidence.claim: The product is Class IIb and PSUR is updated at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: Our product is Class IIb, so we will update the PSUR at least once a year and report the PSUR when the certification authority requires it.

evidence.claim: Traceability records include manufacturing date, lot number, reference number, quantity, and shipment location.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing date, Lot no., Ref. no., quantity and place of shipment are recorded to ensure traceability.
